revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:智賀都神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Chikatsu Shrine Honden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|1=Utsunomiya}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.65716666666667|N|139.8407222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Chikatsu Shrine''' (智賀都神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokujirō Town|ja|徳次郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokujirō Town|1=Tokujira-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11490327}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya City|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine<ref name="tsa">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=581|title=智賀都神社|publisher=栃木県神社庁|accessdate=2020-08-09|qq=}}</ref>. It serves as the guardian shrine for Tokujirō Six Villages and derives its name, "Chikatsu," from the local place name "Chikatsu no Mori" (千勝森)<ref name="tsa"/>. The shrine is believed to have been established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki 9|1=Hōki}} (778 CE) as a branch of {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|qid=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}}<ref name="tsa"/>. The primary deity is {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto), accompanied by {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|タキリビメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}<ref name="tsa"/>. The shrine is believed to grant blessings for abundant harvests, family safety, and good health<ref name="tsa"/>. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it received five koku of shrine lands from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|en|Tokugawa clan|qid=Q1412769|lt=Tokugawa clan|1=Tokugawa clan}} as a gesture of reverence<ref name="tsa"/>. == Festivals == The annual festival is held on {{ill|August 1|en|August 1|qid=Q2788|lt=August 1|1=August 1}}<ref name="tsa"/>. As part of this festival, every three years, the Tokujirō Six Villages parade ornately carved floats ({{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|qid=Q16556730|lt=Yatai|1=Market stall}}) to the shrine in a tradition called "Tsukematsuri" (附祭)<ref name="tsa"/>. The most recent event was held on {{ill|July 26, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 26, 2019|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (evening) and {{ill|July 27, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 27, 2019|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (main festival)<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.utsunomiya-cvb.org/9181.html|title=智賀都神社夏祭りのお知らせ|publisher=宇都宮観光コンベンション協会|accessdate=2020-08-09|qq=}}</ref>. Originally, the event was celebrated on July 31 (eve) and August 1 (main festival), but after interruptions during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, it was revived in {{ill|1946|en|1946|qid=Q18610|lt=Showa 21|1=1946}} and became a triennial event in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=Showa 51|1=1976}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/21383|title=祭りPR 衣装、提灯など展示 徳次郎智賀都神社夏祭り 宇都宮で26日から|author=Harada Takuya|publisher=[[Tokyo Shimbun]]|date=2019-07-03|accessdate=2020-08-09|qq=}}</ref>. Today, the festival takes place on the last Friday (eve) and Saturday (main festival) of July. During the festival, floats from the six villages converge at the shrine after parading through their respective areas. The highlight is a musical performance competition known as "Buttsuke," which elevates the festivities to their peak<ref name="tsa"/>. Additional festivals include the {{ill|11 Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11 Year's Festival|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} (February 3), {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (February 18), {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 26), and Winter Crossing Festival (December 14)<ref name="tsa"/>. == Zelkova Trees == The shrine grounds feature two giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|qid=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees flanking its {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}. Both trees are approximately 40 meters tall and designated as {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|qid=Q23790|lt=Natural Monuments|1=Natural monument}} of Tochigi Prefecture since {{ill|1954|en|1954|qid=Q18581|lt=Showa 29|1=1954}}<ref name="zs">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2343003.htm|title=【けやき】|work=とちぎの文化財|publisher=栃木県教育委員会事務局文化財課|accessdate=2020-08-09|qq=}}</ref>. The eastern tree has a trunk circumference of approximately 8 meters, while the western tree measures around 7.3 meters. Estimated to be about 700 years old, the trees have shown signs of aging and underwent preservation efforts in {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=Heisei 6|1=1994}}<ref name="zs"/>. A similar Zelkova tree known as "Kamito Tokujirō no Keyaki" stands 500 meters northwest of the shrine and is designated as a municipal natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8a%e5%be%b3%e6%ac%a1%e9%83%8e%e3%81%ae%e3%82%b1%e3%83%a4%e3%82%ad/|title=上徳次郎のケヤキ|work=宇都宮の歴史と文化財|publisher=宇都宮市歴史文化資源活用 推進協議会|accessdate=2020-08-09|qq=}}</ref>. == Access == The shrine is located 800 meters north of the Tokujirō intersection on {{ill|Nikkō Kaidō|en|Nikkō Kaidō|qid=Q511576|lt=Nikkō Kaidō|1=Nikkō Kaidō}} (Route 119) and does not have a parking lot<ref name="tsa"/>. By public transportation, it is accessible from {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|qid=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} via a Kanto Bus bound for Nikkō, alighting at Kōyō Chūgakkō-mae and walking for approximately 1 minute<ref name="tsa"/>. == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites|date=2008-09-27|publisher=[[Zuisōsha]]|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Tochigi Historical Walk Editorial Committee|title=Historical Walks in Tochigi Prefecture|series=Historical Walks ⑨|publisher=[[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]|page=350|isbn=978-4-634-24609-6|ref={{sfnref|Tochigi Historical Walk Editorial Committee|2007|qq=}}|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Tochigi Prefecture|1=list of shrines in Tochigi Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11535187}} == External Links == * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/tomiya/chikatujinnjya.html Chikatsu Shrine] - Utsunomiya City Educational Center
{{translated page|ja|智賀都神社|version=103723832|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:智賀都神社]]
